remember: Students will be able to define chemical kinetics and its importance.
Students will be able to recall the factors affecting the rate of a reaction.
Students will be able to remember the different types of reaction orders (zero, first, second).
Students will be able to list the characteristics of a catalyst.
understand: Students will be able to explain the concept of rate of reaction and its units.
Students will be able to describe the relationship between concentration and rate of reaction.
Students will be able to understand the concept of half-life period.
Students will be able to distinguish between homogeneous and heterogeneous catalysis.
apply: Students will be able to apply the integrated rate equations to solve problems.
Students will be able to calculate the rate constant and half-life period of a reaction.
Students will be able to use the Arrhenius equation to determine the activation energy of a reaction.
Students will be able to predict the effect of a catalyst on the rate of a reaction.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the effect of temperature on the rate of a reaction.
Students will be able to compare the characteristics of different types of reactions (reversible, irreversible).
Students will be able to evaluate the role of a catalyst in a reaction mechanism.
Students will be able to interpret the data related to the rate of a reaction and draw meaningful conclusions.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1: Explain the concept of rate of reaction and factors affecting it.
As per NCERT objective 2: Describe the different types of reaction orders and their characteristics.
As per NCERT objective 3: Understand the concept of catalyst and its role in a reaction mechanism.
board_exam_objectives: CBSE tests the ability to solve problems related to rate of reaction and catalyst.
CBSE evaluates the understanding of the concept of half-life period and its relationship with the rate constant.
CBSE assesses the ability to apply the integrated rate equations to solve numerical problems.
CBSE examines the understanding of the effect of temperature on the rate of a reaction and the role of a catalyst.
